what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for the Stepper component: declares a StepItem interface ({label}), a StepperProps interface extending div HTMLAttributes with steps (array of StepItem or string) and an optional active index, and declares the Stepper function plus a default export.
observations: active is an optional number (current step index)
Doc comment describes the three visual states: steps before active are done (gold checkmark dots), active is highlighted, the rest upcoming
Extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> without omitting any handlers (no custom onChange here)
standouts: The doc comment encodes the gold checkmark / highlight / upcoming state model that the .jsx implements
conventions: Paired .d.ts/.jsx typing convention for createElement components
notes: Type contract for a linear numbered stepper; steps are strings or {label}, active marks the current index.
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares exactly the props the .jsx consumes (steps, active).
intention_signals: Gives the buildless Stepper a typed surface so consumers know steps/active and the done/active/upcoming visual semantics.
contribution: Contributes the public type signature of the Stepper progress indicator, including the string-or-{label} step shape and the active-index prop.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declaration for a linear Stepper component with string-or-object steps and an active index prop over a div.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a typed contract for the design-system's numbered linear stepper.
